findings.evidence: docs/vocabulary.md's Epic 2 section defines 'KeystoneAdapter' as a canonical term ('FR-2's instrumentation adapter. Scoped to the scoring half only...'), and the doc's preamble states every term 'should, once implemented, correspond to exactly one typed representation in code (a type, enum, or const)... so a mismatch is a compile/lint error, not something coverage.mjs has to detect after the fact.' The diff adds packages/ai-factory-kaizen/src/adapters/keystone/{types,score,map}.ts but defines no `KeystoneAdapter` type, class, const, or index export anywhere — only two free-standing functions (`runCapabilityScore` in score.ts, `mapKeystoneResult` in map.ts) with no unifying symbol. There is no single compile-checkable representation a reader (or the compiler) can point to as 'the KeystoneAdapter', which is exactly the drift-detection gap vocabulary.md's own governing rule is meant to prevent.
